FAMILY_OUTDOOR_GROUP.rationale: Family of 4-8 people: f/5.6 provides depth across all faces regardless of depth arrangement. 1/250s stops children moving. Continuous shooting captures genuine interactions.
FAMILY_OUTDOOR_GROUP.proTip: Give the family a task rather than asking them to pose — run toward camera, whisper something to each other, walk and hold hands. Genuine interaction always beats forced posing.
FAMILY_OUTDOOR_GROUP.commonMistake: Trying to get everyone to look at the camera simultaneously — this produces stiff, formal images. Capture the family interacting with each other for warmth and authenticity.

NEWBORN_POSED_STUDIO.rationale: Fully controlled studio, consistent artificial light, complete control over all variables. Never use flash on newborns — continuous LED or fluorescent only. Silent electronic shutter prevents startle reflex.
NEWBORN_POSED_STUDIO.proTip: Warm the studio to 26-28C so the baby stays settled during posing. White noise played from a speaker helps maintain settled sleep. Work slowly with an assistant — safety during posing is paramount.
NEWBORN_POSED_STUDIO.commonMistake: Rushing posing setups — a settled newborn can be moved but takes 10-20 minutes to resettle after being disturbed. Patience and slowness produce far better results than working quickly.

TODDLER_CANDID.rationale: Toddlers move fast and unpredictably. High shutter, maximum aperture, and high ISO accept the trade-off of noise for consistently sharp expressions at peak movement.
TODDLER_CANDID.proTip: Get down to the child's eye level. Let the child lead the play and photograph from behind a phone or toy — direct camera attention often stops natural behaviour cold.
TODDLER_CANDID.commonMistake: Trying to direct toddlers — they do not respond to positioning instructions. Follow the child with the camera. Anticipate, do not react.

FAMILY_INDOOR_WINDOW.rationale: Soft directional window light, no flash, warmth and naturalness. The single window creates dimensional lighting that flatters faces while maintaining the warmth of the home environment.
FAMILY_INDOOR_WINDOW.proTip: Position family within 2m of a large window. A white wall or reflector opposite the window fills the shadow side softly. Check white balance — window light shifts throughout the day.
FAMILY_INDOOR_WINDOW.commonMistake: Using fill flash which destroys the natural window light quality and introduces mixed colour temperature. Embrace the natural light direction, use a reflector if fill is needed.

SIBLING_INTERACTION.rationale: Multiple children: f/4 minimum for depth across two faces side by side. 1/320s freezes active play. Faces at different depths require more DOF than single-subject photography.
SIBLING_INTERACTION.proTip: Give siblings a shared activity — building something, looking at a book, running together. The interaction between siblings creates visual relationships that posed shots cannot replicate.
SIBLING_INTERACTION.commonMistake: Shooting at f/2.0 when two children are side by side — the near child is sharp and the far child falls out of focus. f/4 minimum when faces are in the same plane.

MATERNITY_BACKLIT.rationale: Backlit silhouette at golden hour — the bump and form are revealed by side lighting against a bright background. Spot meter the bright background to ensure correct silhouette exposure.
MATERNITY_BACKLIT.proTip: Position the subject so the setting sun is directly behind and to the slightly to the side, creating a rim light. The profile shot reveals the bump form most clearly.
MATERNITY_BACKLIT.commonMistake: Shooting from the front with backlight — this creates an exposed face against a blown background. The profile or three-quarter view is required for the backlit silhouette technique.
